message.content:
Run ALL of the following bash commands in the project directory C:\Users\User\citedby-ai and report EVERY result. This is a read-only audit. Do NOT modify any files.

**Audit 9 — Coherence & Standards:**
```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== Pages sans export default ==="
for f in pages/*.tsx; do
  if ! grep -q "export default" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  $f"
  fi
done
```

```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== Fichiers camelCase dans pages (devrait etre PascalCase) ==="
ls pages/ | grep "^[a-z]"
```

```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== Fichiers snake_case dans services ==="
ls services/ | grep "_"
```

```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== console.log restants (count) ==="
grep -rn "console.log\|console.warn\|console.error" --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" --exclude-dir=node_modules . | grep -v "// debug\|// TODO" | wc -l
```

```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== Details console.log par fichier ==="
grep -rn "console.log\|console.warn\|console.error" --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" --exclude-dir=node_modules . | grep -v "// debug\|// TODO" | sed 's/:.*/:/' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -15
```

```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== Commentaires (sample) ==="
grep -rn "// " --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" --exclude-dir=node_modules . | head -30
```

Report ALL results verbatim.

content.text:
I am unable to complete this audit because Bash access has been denied. All six commands in this audit require Bash execution to:

1. **Pages sans export default** -- Loop through `pages/*.tsx` and grep each file for `export default`
2. **Fichiers camelCase dans pages** -- List files in `pages/` and filter for lowercase-starting names
3. **Fichiers snake_case dans services** -- List files in `services/` and filter for underscore-containing names
4. **console.log count** -- Recursively grep all `.ts`/`.tsx` files for console statements and count them
5. **console.log details by file** -- Same grep but grouped and sorted by file with occurrence counts
6. **Comments sample** -- Grep for `// ` comment patterns across all TypeScript files

All of these are read-only operations (no file modifications). They rely on shell constructs like `for` loops, `grep`, `wc`, `sed`, `sort`, `uniq`, and piping, which can only be executed via Bash.

**To proceed**, please grant Bash permission so I can run these audit commands. Alternatively, if there is a security policy in effect, you could run the six commands manually in your terminal and share the output with me for analysis.
